ampere_law.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: electric currents conjure the magnetic fields inside every motor on Earth.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Ampère's Law

Magnetic fields don't just appear — electric currents CREATE them. Ampère's Law reveals the deep link between electricity and magnetism that powers every motor on Earth. ⚡🧲

angular_acceleration.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: how quickly something's spin rate changes — the skater's arm-tuck trick.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Angular Acceleration

Ever wonder why a spinning figure skater speeds up when they pull in their arms? It all comes down to angular acceleration — rotational motion's most dramatic secret. 🌀

angular_momentum.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: the conserved spin currency of planets, galaxies and ice skaters.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Angular Momentum

The universe conserves angular momentum — it's why planets orbit, galaxies spiral, and ice skaters spin faster. One equation governs them all. 🌍💫

beer_lambert_law.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: measure a solution's concentration just by shining light through it.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Beer-Lambert Law

How do scientists measure the concentration of a solution just by shining light through it? The Beer-Lambert Law turns light into a precision instrument. 🔬💡

bragg_law.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: bounce X-rays off crystal planes and map atoms no microscope can see.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Bragg's Law

How do we 'see' atoms when they're far too small for any microscope? Bragg's Law lets us use X-rays to map the hidden architecture of crystals. 💎🔬

capacitance.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: the art of storing electric charge — your touchscreen runs on it.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Capacitance

Every phone, circuit, and power grid relies on one deceptively simple concept: the ability to store electric charge. This is capacitance — the battery's quieter cousin. ⚡📱

centripetal_force.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: the inward pull that keeps you in your seat through a rollercoaster loop.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Centripetal Force

What stops you flying off a rollercoaster loop? What keeps the Moon orbiting Earth? The same force — centripetal force — the invisible hand of circular motion. 🎢🌕

coefficient_of_friction.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: the grip number that decides whether your shoes slip or stick.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Coefficient of Friction

Friction is why we can walk, drive, and grip things — and why machines wear out. The coefficient of friction puts a number on one of physics' most everyday forces. 👟⚙️

continuity_equation.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: why a river runs faster through a narrow gorge — mass must be conserved.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Continuity Equation

Why does a river flow faster through a narrow gorge? The continuity equation is conservation of mass in fluid form — elegant, simple, and everywhere in nature. 🌊🏞️

coulombs_law.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: gravity's mathematical twin for electric charges — but up to 10³⁶ times stronger.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Coulomb's Law

The electric force between charges follows the exact same mathematical structure as gravity — but can be 10³⁶ times stronger. Coulomb's Law is electrostatics at its finest. ⚡🔋

density.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: the mass-per-volume idea behind floating ice and city-sized dead stars.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Density (ρ = m/V)

One of the simplest equations in physics hides some of the universe's biggest secrets — from why ice floats to how neutron stars cram a Sun's worth of mass into a city. 🧊⭐

doppler_effect.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: one idea explains a passing siren's dropping pitch AND redshifted galaxies.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Doppler Effect

The sound of a racing car drops in pitch as it passes you. Galaxies move away from us because their light is redshifted. One effect — the Doppler Effect — explains both. 🚗🌌

elastic_potential_energy.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: the power a drawn bow quietly stores before the arrow flies.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Elastic Potential Energy

Every stretched spring, every drawn bow, every bouncing ball stores energy invisibly. Elastic potential energy is nature's way of saving power for later. 🏹🌀

electric_power.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: the rate a circuit delivers the goods — kettles and national grids obey it.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Electric Power (P = IV)

P = IV. Three symbols. Every power station, every circuit, every LED bulb on Earth obeys this equation. Electric power is the lifeblood of modern civilisation. 💡⚡

escape_velocity.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: 11.2 km/s — the magic number that unchains you from Earth forever.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Escape Velocity

To leave Earth forever, you need to hit exactly 11.2 km/s. No more, no less — that's escape velocity. The equation that turned space travel from dream to reality. 🚀🌍

faraday_law.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: a changing magnetic field births electricity — every generator's secret.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Faraday's Law

Every generator on Earth works because of one discovery: a changing magnetic field creates electricity. Faraday's Law literally powers the modern world. ⚡🔄

first_law_thermodynamics.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: energy can only change costume — it never appears or vanishes.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: First Law of Thermodynamics

Energy cannot be created or destroyed — only transformed. The First Law of Thermodynamics is perhaps the most important statement in all of science. 🔥🔋

frequency_period.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: the how-often and the how-long of every wave — each is 1 over the other.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Frequency and Period

Frequency and period are two sides of the same coin — one tells you how fast, the other tells you how long. Together they describe every wave in the universe. 〰️🌊

gauss_law.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: total flux out of any closed surface equals the charge trapped inside.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Gauss's Law

Gauss's Law might be the most elegant equation in electrostatics — it connects the total electric flux through any closed surface directly to the charge inside. Pure genius. 🔵⚡

gravity_law.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: the falling apple and the orbiting Moon obey one and the same rule.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation

An apple falls from a tree. The Moon stays in orbit. Newton realised these were the SAME phenomenon — and wrote the equation that unified the heavens and the Earth. 🍎🌕

inductance.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: inertia, but for electric current — transformers depend on it.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Inductance

An inductor resists changes in electric current — it's like inertia, but for electricity. Without inductance, every transformer and wireless charger would be impossible. 🔄⚡

length_contraction.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: near light speed, space itself squeezes along your direction of travel.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Length Contraction

At near-light speed, space itself compresses in the direction of travel. Length contraction isn't science fiction — it's Special Relativity, and it's very real. 🚀📏

lorentz_factor.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: the γ that blows up to infinity as you chase the speed of light.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Lorentz Factor

The Lorentz factor (γ) is where Special Relativity hides all its drama. As you approach the speed of light, γ shoots toward infinity — and physics gets wonderfully weird. ∞🚀

lorentz_force.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: the sideways shove magnetic fields give moving charges — motors live on it.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Lorentz Force

A moving charge in a magnetic field gets pushed sideways. This bizarre perpendicular force — the Lorentz force — drives every electric motor and particle accelerator on Earth. ⚡🧲

magnetic_force_wire.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: put amps through metal inside a field and the metal jumps — motors, in one line.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Magnetic Force on a Current-Carrying Wire

Run current through a wire inside a magnetic field and it MOVES. This is the entire principle behind every electric motor ever built. Physics put to work. ⚡🔧

moment_of_inertia.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: rotation's stand-in for mass — flywheel designers obsess over it.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Moment of Inertia

Moment of inertia is 'mass' for rotation — it tells you how hard it is to spin something up or slow it down. Engineers obsess over it for everything from flywheels to figure skating. 🌀⚙️

momentum.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: the quantity the universe refuses to lose in any collision or explosion.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Momentum (p = mv)

p = mv. The most important quantity in collisions, explosions, and rocket science. Conservation of momentum is a law the universe NEVER breaks. 💥🚀

newtons_second_law.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: the three-letter law the whole of classical mechanics stands on.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Newton's Second Law

F = ma. Three letters that launched the scientific revolution. Newton's Second Law is the cornerstone of classical mechanics and the most famous equation after E=mc². 🍎⚡

ohms_law.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: V, I and R in one line — the alphabet of every circuit ever built.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Ohm's Law

V = IR. If you understand this one equation, you understand the fundamentals of every electrical circuit ever built. Ohm's Law is the alphabet of electronics. 🔌💡

photoelectric_effect.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: light kicking electrons out of metal — the Nobel Einstein actually won.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Photoelectric Effect

Einstein won the Nobel Prize for THIS — not relativity. Light kicks electrons out of metal, proving light comes in packets. The photoelectric effect launched quantum mechanics. 💡⚛️

poiseuille_law.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: why halving an artery's width slashes the blood flow sixteen-fold.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Poiseuille's Law

Why does halving the width of an artery reduce blood flow by 16×? Poiseuille's Law governs how fluids move through pipes — and it's the key to understanding cardiovascular medicine. 🩸🔬

potential_energy.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: what a boulder perched on a cliff edge is silently saving up.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Gravitational Potential Energy

Every object held at a height is silently storing energy, waiting to be released. Gravitational potential energy is stored patience — and it shapes everything from dams to roller coasters. ⛰️🎢

power_formula.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: not how much work you do — how FAST you do it.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Power (P = W/t)

Power isn't just about how much work you do — it's about how fast you do it. The power formula is what separates a sprinter from a marathon runner, and a sports car from a truck. 🏎️⚡

pressure_formula.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: why a stiletto heel dents a floor that an elephant's foot won't.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Pressure (P = F/A)

Pressure is force spread over an area — which is why a stiletto heel does more damage to a floor than an elephant's foot. Simple equation, profound consequences. 👠🐘

radioactive_decay.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: perfectly random for one atom, perfectly predictable for a trillion.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Radioactive Decay Law

Radioactive decay is perfectly random at the quantum level — yet in bulk, it follows a precise mathematical law. Half-lives power everything from carbon dating to nuclear medicine. ☢️🌍

rc_time_constant.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: the heartbeat of a charging capacitor — it sets how fast a camera flash recovers.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: RC Time Constant (τ = RC)

The RC time constant determines how fast a capacitor charges and discharges. It's the heartbeat of timing circuits — from camera flashes to the rhythm of your digital devices. ⏱️🔋

relativistic_energy.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: the equation on every physics T-shirt — matter, unmasked.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Mass-Energy Equivalence (E = mc²)

E = mc². The most famous equation in history tells us that mass IS energy — frozen, concentrated, waiting to be unleashed. Relativity rewrote our understanding of reality itself. 💥🌌

relativistic_momentum.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: push as hard as you like — this is why nothing overtakes light.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Relativistic Momentum

At everyday speeds, p = mv works fine. But near the speed of light, momentum grows toward infinity — no matter how hard you push. Relativistic momentum is why c is the cosmic speed limit. 🚀∞

resistance_formula.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: why fat copper wires feed your house while thin ones glow in your toaster.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Resistance (R = ρL/A)

Resistance isn't futile — it's fundamental. The resistance formula explains why thick copper wires power your home and thin nichrome wires glow in your toaster. 🔌🍞

rotational_kinetic_energy.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: the invisible stockpile inside every spinning flywheel and rolling wheel.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Rotational Kinetic Energy

A spinning flywheel stores enormous energy invisibly. Rotational kinetic energy is the rotational twin of regular kinetic energy — and it's why tops spin, wheels roll, and planets rotate. 🌍⚙️

rydberg_formula.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: the pre-quantum recipe that nailed hydrogen's rainbow of spectral lines.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Rydberg Formula

Before quantum mechanics existed, Rydberg cracked the code of hydrogen's spectral lines with a single elegant formula. It was the first crack in the wall of classical physics. 🌈⚛️

schrodinger_equation.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: it won't say where the particle is — only where it might be (ask the cat).
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Schrödinger Equation

The Schrödinger Equation doesn't tell you where a particle IS — it tells you where it might be. It's the master equation of quantum mechanics, and reality gets strange from here. 🐱⚛️

stefan_boltzmann_law.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: double a star's temperature and you get sixteen times the glow.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Stefan-Boltzmann Law

Double the temperature of a star and it radiates 16× more energy. The Stefan-Boltzmann Law governs how every hot object glows — from a candle flame to a supernova. 🌟🔥

thermal_expansion.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: the reason bridges have gaps and railway tracks buckle in a heatwave.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Thermal Expansion

Bridges have expansion gaps. Railway tracks buckle in summer. Thermometers work by expansion. Thermal expansion is one of the most quietly consequential equations in engineering. 🌡️🌉

time_dilation.caption:
⏳ Countdown's on. Hint: GPS satellites' clocks drift — Einstein told us exactly how much to correct.
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Time Dilation

Time passes slower for someone moving fast or sitting in a strong gravitational field. GPS satellites would be useless without correcting for time dilation. Einstein was right. ⏳🛰️

torque.caption:
⏳ Beat the countdown. Hint: the leverage law: why door handles sit far from the hinge.
Drop your guess below before the timer hits zero — no cheating ⏳

ANSWER: Torque

Torque is the rotational version of force — it's why a long wrench loosens a stubborn bolt and why door handles are placed far from the hinge. Leverage is physics made practical. 🔧🚪

velocity_formula.caption:
⏳ Three seconds on the clock. Hint: speed with a direction attached — the difference changes everything.
Type what you think it is in the comments, then watch the reveal 👇

ANSWER: Velocity (v = d/t)

Speed tells you how fast. Velocity tells you how fast AND in which direction. That difference changes everything in physics — from projectile motion to relativity. 🏃➡️

wave_equation.caption:
⏳ Quick — before the timer runs out. Hint: sound, light and ripples on a pond all march to this single PDE.
Comment your guess before the answer drops — how fast did you get it? 💬

ANSWER: Wave Equation

Sound, light, water ripples, quantum particles — they're all described by the wave equation. One mathematical structure governs the oscillating heartbeat of the universe. 🌊🔊

work_energy.caption:
⏳ No pausing allowed. Hint: every joule you put in shows up, exactly, in the object's ½mv².
Comment your answer before the reveal — and no scrubbing forward 👀

ANSWER: Work-Energy Theorem

Work is force applied over a distance — and every joule of work done changes the kinetic energy of an object by exactly the same amount. The work-energy theorem is physics keeping score. 💪⚡
